title: peucedanum teretifolium
intro: Peucedanum teretifolium, also known as Rough Chervil or Rough Frill, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits associated with Peucedanum teretifolium include relief from digestive problems, reduction of fever, and treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
